channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Ratnaveer Engineering Plans ₹338 Crore Copper Laminates Project
item.description: Ratnaveer Precision Engineering is starting a ₹338 crore copper clad laminates (CCL) project in Vadodara, Gujarat, with commercial operations planned for November 2026 under the Electronics Component Manufacturing Scheme (ECMS). This diversification aims to boost India's domestic electronics manufacturing and self-reliance. The project includes significant machinery orders and ongoing civil construction, positioning Ratnaveer within the nation's growing electronics sector.

item.title: India Pharma Faces Supply Woes Despite Duty Waivers
item.description: India's pharmaceutical sector continues to face supply chain risks for vital petrochemical inputs, despite the government waiving customs duties on over 40 products until June 30. Industry executives emphasize that securing timely shipments, not just lower costs, is the main challenge. Saudi chemical giant SABIC’s force majeure declaration on key materials highlights these supply concerns amid rising global volatility.

item.title: India Cracks Down on Pharma Retail with CCTV, Data Rules
item.description: India's Drug Consultative Committee (DCC) now requires all medical stores to install CCTV cameras, making every sale verifiable. This move aims to enhance surveillance against unregulated drug sales and protect child safety. A proposed national drug information system will also centralize data for real-time tracking of controlled substances. These initiatives aim for greater transparency and regulatory control in India's pharmacy network, but raise concerns about compliance costs and data infrastructure.

item.title: HDFC Bank Faces Probe Demand After Chairman Quits Over Ethics
item.description: The All India Bank Employees' Association (AIBEA) is calling for a government investigation into HDFC Bank's operations and governance. This demand comes after non-executive chairman Atanu Chakraborty resigned, citing ethical concerns. The union wants assurance for the bank's 120 million customers and shareholders. This situation adds to growing investor unease about governance in India's banking sector.

item.title: EESL Debt Forces Sale of IntelliSmart Amid India Smart Meter Delays
item.description: IntelliSmart Infrastructure, co-owned by NIIF and EESL, is in early talks to be acquired for about $700 million. The sale is driven by Energy Efficiency Services Ltd (EESL)'s ₹6,045 crore debt (as of March 2025). Despite strong government support for smart meters via RDSS, the sector faces significant installation delays and cost risks, though investor interest remains high.

item.title: India Airlines Get Policy Break: Free Seat Mandate Rolled Back
item.description: The Indian government has suspended a mandate requiring airlines to offer 60% of seats for free selection. This decision, driven by industry opposition and concerns over fare structures, provides immediate financial relief to carriers. While the sector faces pressures like rising fuel costs and currency depreciation, this rollback allows airlines to better manage revenue and navigate market dynamics, especially for dominant players like IndiGo.

item.title: RBL Bank Hits ₹2.5 Lakh Crore Business Mark in FY26
item.description: RBL Bank has reached a major financial milestone, crossing ₹2.5 lakh crore in total business by the end of FY26. This represents a 24% growth year-over-year. The bank saw deposits climb 25% to ₹1.39 lakh crore and advances grow 22% to ₹1.15 lakh crore, with key liquidity and collection metrics remaining strong.

item.title: HDFC Bank Plans Debt Raise Amid Governance Concerns and Stock Slump
item.description: HDFC Bank's board will meet April 18 to consider raising funds through debt instruments. This plan comes as the bank faces growing governance scrutiny following former chairman Atanu Chakraborty's resignation over ethical concerns. Despite analyst optimism for upcoming earnings, the bank's stock remains near 52-week lows, showing investor unease about leadership stability.

item.title: Trishakti Industries Tops Capex Goals, Stock Gains 2.6%
item.description: Trishakti Industries acquired Rs 20 crore in new equipment, raising its total capital spending to Rs 258 crore. This spending greatly exceeds its Rs 100 crore FY26 guidance, with around Rs 210 crore already spent this fiscal year. The company's stock closed up 2.63% on April 2, 2026, at Rs 136.35. The new equipment is expected to generate Rs 6 crore in revenue over the next year.

item.title: Safety Controls IPO: Debt Repayment Raises Investor Questions Amid Sector Boom
item.description: Safety Controls & Devices launches its ₹48 crore IPO on April 6. Funds will go mainly to working capital (₹31.5 crore) and debt repayment (₹6 crore). While the company has a ₹139 crore order book, its allocation towards debt servicing prompts questions. The IPO values the firm around 17.64x its post-issue P/E, within a booming infrastructure sector fueled by government spending. Key risks include reliance on government contracts and client concentration.

item.title: IndianOil Ramps Up Karnataka LPG Supply Amid Private Station Closures
item.description: Indian Oil Corporation (IndianOil) has sharply increased auto LPG supplies in Karnataka to meet demand surge after private dispensing stations closed. This action underscores the vital role of Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs) in ensuring fuel availability and points to a possible long-term gain in market share for reliable state-run companies. Daily sales at IndianOil stations have jumped significantly, showing the market's dependence on its strong infrastructure.

item.title: CAG: IPPB's UPI Failures, Data Lapses Threaten Inclusion Goals
item.description: A recent Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) report has sharply criticized India Post Payments Bank (IPPB) for significant operational deficiencies. These include troublingly high technical decline rates and extensive downtime for its Unified Payments Interface (UPI) services, well above regulatory limits and competitor performance. The audit also uncovered serious lapses in account opening processes, like using unverified mobile numbers and linking multiple customer files to one number, raising major data integrity and security concerns. These issues undermine customer trust and hinder IPPB's fundamental mandate of promoting financial inclusion.

item.title: Oracle Cuts 12,000 India Jobs in Major AI Infrastructure Push
item.description: Oracle is cutting about 12,000 jobs in India as part of a global effort to invest billions in AI infrastructure. This strategic shift comes amid tough competition from cloud rivals and economic pressures like a falling rupee and inflation. While rivals are hiring more AI talent, Oracle's job cuts signal its race to reallocate resources for generative AI leadership.

item.title: GR Infraprojects Secures ₹6,200 Cr in New Orders; Execution Now Key
item.description: GR Infraprojects Ltd. has boosted its order backlog by securing contracts totaling over ₹6,200 crore across railway, highway, and energy storage projects. Key wins include a ₹1,898 crore deal with West Central Railway and significant highway projects from NHAI. This large influx of new business has investors closely watching the company's execution capabilities and potential margin pressures.

item.title: Coinbase Wins OCC Charter for Federal Crypto Custody
item.description: Coinbase has secured preliminary approval from the U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) for a national trust company charter. This conditional green light is a significant step toward operating as a federally regulated crypto custodian. The move supports Coinbase’s strategy to build steadier revenue streams from custody services, which institutional investors increasingly seek for secure asset holding.

item.title: CAG Hits Airtel With ₹8.49 Crore Penalty for Rural Project Delays
item.description: Bharti Airtel faces a ₹8.49 crore penalty from the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) for significant delays in a rural telecom project in Assam and Sikkim. The project, managed by the Digital Bharat Nidhi (DBN) fund, has been plagued by contract enforcement issues. This comes as Airtel maintains its position as the world's second-largest operator, highlighting a contrast between its vast global reach and specific project execution.

item.title: NTPC Sets FY26 Record Capacity, Bets on Renewables Amid Market Swings
item.description: State-owned NTPC achieved record capacity additions of 9,619 MW in fiscal year 2025-26, including 5,488 MW of renewable energy. The company also saw strong growth in coal production and power trading. Despite operational successes, NTPC's stock is experiencing volatility, trading around ₹359.65 with a market cap of approximately ₹3.53 lakh crore. The company is aggressively pursuing diversification into areas like battery storage, green hydrogen, and nuclear power, signaling a strategic transformation that analysts are evaluating with mixed outlooks.

item.title: India Fuel Tax Shake-Up: State Refiners Gain, Exporters Squeezed
item.description: India's government has changed fuel taxes, cutting excise duties and reinstating export taxes on diesel and jet fuel. This move is set to boost profits for state-run Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) like IOCL, BPCL, and HPCL by an estimated $12-$20 per barrel. However, refiners focused on exports, such as MRPL and CPCL, will see their margins shrink. Numaligarh Refinery Ltd (NRL) expects a $32.5 per barrel hit. Reliance Industries Ltd's export refinery is exempt, but its domestic operations will be affected. The policy shift carries an estimated fiscal cost of ₹1.65 lakh crore.

item.title: India Directs Feedstock Amid Geopolitical Strain; RIL SEZ Tax Exemption Confirmed
item.description: India's government is directing refineries to reallocate C3 and C4 petrochemical feedstocks from LPG production to the pharmaceutical and food sectors due to shortages worsened by geopolitical tensions impacting the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, Reliance Industries' Special Economic Zone (SEZ) refinery has been clarified as exempt from new windfall taxes on diesel and jet fuel exports, partially shielding its export-oriented operations. This comes as India assures domestic fuel availability despite rising global crude prices and costs for oil marketing companies.

item.title: Sathlokhar Synergys Lands ₹125 Cr Orders; Growth Amid Margin Worries
item.description: Sathlokhar Synergys E&C Global began the fiscal year securing ₹125 crore in new orders, highlighted by a ₹104 crore project from Reliance Consumer Products. The company projects over 100% turnover growth for FY26, focusing on infrastructure and solar EPC. Yet, its aggressive expansion, past stock declines, and thin analyst coverage raise questions about its margin health and operational capacity. The ₹1,097 crore order book offers near-term revenue visibility.

item.title: Punjab Carbonic Seeks IPO to Boost Carbon Capture and Ethanol Growth
item.description: Punjab Carbonic, a carbon capture firm, has filed for an Initial Public Offering (IPO) with India's SEBI. The company aims to raise capital by selling 95 lakh equity shares, including 60 lakh new shares and 35 lakh from existing shareholders. Funds will be used for new CO recovery units, logistics, ethanol distillery expansion, debt repayment, and general corporate purposes. The shares are planned for listing on both the NSE and BSE.

item.title: CFS Pivots: Sells Magnet Tech to Fund Its Fusion Future
item.description: Commonwealth Fusion Systems (CFS) is now selling its advanced high-temperature superconducting magnets, including its largest-ever deal with Realta Fusion. This pivot uses its magnet-making expertise to generate revenue, funding its own fusion reactors like SPARC and ARC, and supplying components to companies like Type One Energy. Having raised nearly $3 billion, CFS aims to be a crucial technology provider for the rapidly growing fusion energy field.

item.title: Raymond Realty Records Strong Sales Surge, But Collections Decline
item.description: Raymond Realty posted record pre-sales of ₹3,023 crore in FY26, boosted by a 139% Q4 surge. However, annual collections declined 9% to ₹1,725 crore, pointing to cash flow challenges. The company is also growing its ₹43,000 crore development pipeline with ₹605 crore net debt, while investors weigh the strong sales against collection figures.

item.title: Lahori Zeera's ₹10 Drinks Challenge Coke & Pepsi in India
item.description: Lahori Zeera, founded in 2017, has rapidly grown in India's ₹60,000 crore beverage market, challenging global giants Coca-Cola and PepsiCo. The brand focuses on traditional Indian flavors and an accessible ₹10 price point, reportedly achieving over ₹500 crore in revenue and targeting ₹1,000 crore. Success, driven by funding and a strong distribution network to kirana stores, is forcing established players to adjust their pricing.

item.title: PNB's Loan Growth Soars Amid Margin Pressure, Cheaper Stock
item.description: Punjab National Bank announced strong March quarter growth in loans and deposits, with total business up 10.79% year-on-year. Gross advances rose 12.97% and deposits 9.25%, while the CASA ratio hit 73.70% and gross NPAs fell to 3.19%. However, the bank expects Net Interest Margins (NIMs) to dip slightly in FY26 to 2.8-2.9% from 2.9-3% in FY25. PNB also trades at a much lower Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio than its large public sector banking rivals.

item.title: RVNL Bags ₹242 Cr Rail Contract Amid Analyst Sell Ratings
item.description: Rail Vikas Nigam Ltd (RVNL) won a ₹242.49 crore contract from South Central Railway for overhead equipment upgrades. The 24-month project adds to RVNL's order book. However, the announcement comes as RVNL shares trade near their 52-week low, with a high P/E ratio and analyst 'Sell' ratings, signaling investor concerns despite new business.

item.title: Rama Steel Tubes: Q4 Volume Dip Against Sector Boom Sparks Valuation Debate
item.description: Rama Steel Tubes Ltd (RSTL) reported a 21.9% year-on-year drop in Q4 FY26 sales volume to 0.43 lakh tonnes, contrasting with a 10.2% full-year growth to 2.15 lakh tonnes. While the annual performance shows resilience, the quarterly contraction occurs against a backdrop of robust sector demand driven by infrastructure spending. The company's current valuation metrics suggest potential concerns when benchmarked against industry peers and sector growth prospects.

item.title: India Surpasses FY26 Indirect Tax Target; FY27 Faces New Fiscal Challenges
item.description: India's indirect tax revenue for fiscal year 2026 surpassed government targets, with customs, excise, and central GST collections exceeding expectations. However, a new health and national security cess fell far short of its goal. This performance, alongside planned tax cuts and global economic concerns, is leading officials to revise revenue expectations for FY27, which faces significant fiscal uncertainties.

item.title: CapitaLand Investment Boosts India Data Centers, Logistics
item.description: CapitaLand Investment (CLI) is sharply increasing its focus on India, building a 23 million sq ft development pipeline for logistics and data centers. The company is using its listed trust, CapitaLand India Trust (CLINT), to recycle capital through asset sales and purchases to fund this expansion. CLINT's portfolio was valued at S$3.8 billion as of December 2025, and CLI holds a 25% stake, showing a clear shift to new-economy assets and digital infrastructure in India.

item.title: India Microfinance Sees Digital Surge, Informal Lending Drops to 1%
item.description: India's microfinance sector is transforming, with informal borrowing now at just 1% in FY25. Digital loan disbursement is 100%, and over 75% of loans fund income-generating activities. This shift shows borrowers favor formal, regulated credit with competitive rates. Borrower confidence is strong, highlighting the sector's growing support for livelihoods. The sector is now recalibrating for quality lending and risk management, balancing growth with stability.

item.title: Bank of India's Growth Challenged by RBI Forex Moves and Provisional Data
item.description: Bank of India (BoI) reported preliminary Q4 FY26 results showing double-digit growth in global business, deposits, and advances, reaching ₹16.98 lakh crore. This operational growth, fueled by the RAM portfolio, relies on provisional figures pending auditor review. However, the positive numbers face pressure from wider market issues, including recent Reserve Bank of India (RBI) forex defense actions that led to stock drops on April 2, 2026. Analysts hold a 'Buy' rating, but near-term challenges remain.

item.title: Punjab & Sind Bank: Loan Growth Strains Funding Amid Cost Rise
item.description: Punjab & Sind Bank reported strong Q4 figures with 18.39% gross advance growth and 12.37% total deposit increase. However, a marginal dip in the CASA ratio to 30.77% from 31.43% suggests a shift towards more expensive funding. This trend, amplified by a widening credit-deposit gap across the sector, raises concerns over Net Interest Margin pressure. The bank's stock continues to hover near 52-week lows, reflecting investor caution.

item.title: Crypto's Fragmented Systems Cost Institutions Billions
item.description: Institutions are increasingly adopting digital assets, but their systems are scattered across custodians, exchanges, and other firms. This patchwork causes major operational delays and wastes capital. With over $680 billion in digital assets under professional custody, these disconnected systems hinder trading, hedging, and managing cash. There's a growing need for connected, working-together platforms, especially as core functions like custody, asset tokenization, and stablecoin settlement become more important. Firms are now focused on smooth connections to save money and get ahead in the fast-changing digital asset world.

item.title: Franklin Templeton: Don't Cancel SIPs, Buy Low for Wealth
item.description: Franklin Templeton India is urging investors not to halt their Systematic Investment Plans (SIPs) amid market volatility. Chief Avinash Satwalekar explains that cancelling SIPs means missing the chance to buy more units at lower prices, crucial for long-term wealth creation through counter-cyclical investing. The firm has also introduced a new equity long-short fund to help mitigate downside risk.

item.title: RBI Holds Rates Amid Inflation Fears from West Asia Conflict
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is expected to hold its key repo rate at 5.25% on April 8. Rising tensions in West Asia have pushed oil prices over $100 a barrel, increasing inflation worries and making the rupee unstable. The RBI aims to control prices while supporting economic growth. This meeting is likely to confirm a long pause in interest rate cuts.

item.title: India-China Trade Talks: Deficit, WTO Disputes Cloud Diplomacy
item.description: India's Commerce Minister met China's counterpart for trade talks. Despite diplomatic steps to expand trade, a record $100 billion deficit, WTO disputes, and infrastructure delays show deep economic challenges remain, even as some investment rules are eased.

item.title: CapitaLand Investment Expands India Logistics, Data Centers
item.description: CapitaLand Investment (CLI) is aggressively expanding its Indian operations, focusing on logistics and data centers with a 23 million sq ft development pipeline planned over the next three to four years. As detailed in its FY25 annual report, this strategy includes earmarking nine million sq ft for logistics and significantly increasing data center capacity. The expansion is powered by active capital recycling through its listed entity, CapitaLand India Trust (CLINT), which has made key forward purchases and divestments to capitalize on India's growing digital economy and evolving supply chains.

item.title: JLR Q4 Sales Jump 61% on Luxury Models, But Annual Decline Continues
item.description: Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) saw wholesale volumes jump 61.1% in Q4 FY26, recovering production after a cyber incident. Yet, year-on-year volumes dropped 14.5% in Q4 and 23.2% for the full year due to market challenges, U.S. tariffs, and model shifts. Strong sales of premium models like Range Rover, Range Rover Sport, and Defender (77.1% of Q4 volumes) are crucial for profitability despite fewer units sold. Tata Motors shares closed slightly up on April 2, 2026, as the luxury auto market faces geopolitical issues and changing EV demand.

item.title: Bharti Airtel Fuels AI Data Center Growth with $1B Nxtra Funding
item.description: Bharti Airtel is injecting significant capital into its data center arm, Nxtra, securing $1 billion from investors including Alpha Wave Global and Carlyle to boost AI-ready infrastructure. This strategic move aims to scale Nxtra's capacity to 1 GW, targeting a 25% market share. The investment complements Bharti's steady telecom operations, growing African business, and anticipated ARPU increases, while the company also focuses on deleveraging its balance sheet.

item.title: Mahindra Bets Big on EVs Post-Asset Sale, Faces Market Headwinds
item.description: Mahindra & Mahindra, led by Anish Shah, has divested underperforming assets to invest in electric vehicles (EVs) and renewables. Its core SUV and tractor businesses gained market share in FY25, and M&M Financial Services improved asset quality. Despite these gains, the company navigates intense EV competition and rising economic uncertainties.
